title: Small Business Lending Fund
summary: The Small Business Lending Fund (SBLF) is a legacy program established by the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to provide capital to community banks and community development loan funds to encourage small business lending. Treasury invested over $4.0 billion across 332 institutions. As of Q3 2025, the cumulative net impact on qualified small business lending over baseline is $19.1 billion. This is an informational page about a historical government lending program, not a current macro catalyst.
key_points: SBLF was established by the Small Business Jobs Act of 2010 to encourage small business lending through community banks
Treasury invested $4.0 billion in 332 institutions ($3.9B in 281 community banks, $104M in 51 CDLFs)
Institutions operate in over 3,000 locations across 47 states and DC
Cumulative net impact on qualified small business lending as of Q3 2025: $19.1 billion over baseline
Baseline is the average of four calendar quarters ended June 30, 2010
As of December 1, 2025, 327 institutions with $3.95B aggregate investments have fully redeemed and exited the program
why_it_matters: This page describes a historical government lending program that is now largely winding down. It does not represent a current macro catalyst as it discusses a legacy program from 2010 rather than new policy actions, data releases, or market-moving events. Most institutions have already exited the program.
important_numbers: $19.1 billion cumulative net impact on QSBL (as of Q3 2025)
